audit_items.proof_trace: The only runtime function-pointer dereference is `s_log_handler` at line 74, which is initialized to the non-NULL `default_log_handler` (line 68) and protected by `set_log_handler`'s guard (lines 85-86) that substitutes `null_log_handler` for any NULL input, so `s_log_handler` can never be NULL at the dereference site. The remaining pointer parameters (`fmt`, `filename`) in `default_log_handler` always receive non-NULL compile-time string literals via the `SLOG`/`SLOG_IF` macros (`__FILE__` and string literal `fmt`), so no NULL dereference is possible on any reachable code path.
